title: A Mutation within the β Subunit of Escherichia coli RNA Polymerase Impairs Transcription from Bacteriophage T4 Middle Promoters
summary: The paper discusses bacteriophage T4 and its mutants, focusing on their interactions with E. coli host RNA polymerase, particularly the effects of mutations in the host's rpoB gene on phage growth and transcription.

extra.interaction_with_host: σ 70, the specificity subunit of RNA polymerase

extra.growth_phenotype: grows poorly in wild-type E. coli
extra.restricted_growth: does not grow in the E. coli mutant strain TabG
extra.mutation_effect: G1249D mutation in host rpoB restricts growth

extra.growth_restriction: restricted growth in E. coli B strain TabG at nonpermissive temperature

extra.growth_phenotype: produces small plaques on wild-type E. coli
extra.restricted_growth: no detectable plaques on E. coli mutant strains TabG and B11
extra.mutation_effect: G1249D mutation in host rpoB restricts growth
